Done. Here's a summary of what was built and verified:

**`pc archeologist --codex`** â€” scans `~/.codex/sessions/` + `~/.codex/archived_sessions/` recursively for `rollout-*.jsonl` files. These already speak the `session_meta` + `response_item` wire format the transcript parser knows, so no synthesis step is needed. Found **221 projects / 2788 sessions** on this machine. Legacy `rollout-*.json` files (441, no cwd field) are counted and skipped with a message.

**`pc archeologist --opencode`** â€” reads `~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db` (SQLite), queries sessions by directory, and synthesizes flat JSONL from `message` + `part` tables using the same preamble pattern as `--tenex`. I generated 3 test sessions with `opencode run` to validate the pipeline; they show up correctly as **3 opencode projects**.

Both flags integrate with every existing flag: `--project`, `--since`, `--dry-run`, `--output-dir`, picker, dedup ledger. Display names get `[codex]` / `[opencode]` tags. 113 tests pass including 6 new ones covering file classification, recursive walk, cwd-less skip, grouping, JSONL synthesis, and XML skipping. Pushed directly to master.
Committed and pushed â€” README rewrite, `3756bae`.

it shouldn't take --codex and --opencode -- instead it should try to load from all known paths at the same time, so if I run `pc archeologist` it should try to detect claude code, tenex, opencode, codex conversations and show their source on the selection tui

Done. `pc archeologist` now auto-detects and merges Claude Code, TENEX, Codex, and opencode sessions on every run â€” no flags needed. Sources that aren't installed are skipped silently; those that are found print a brief count line before the picker opens.
Nothing staged â€” the untracked files are docs, obsidian config, and a swap file, none of which look like code changes to commit. Nothing to do here.
